artifact_reviewed: protocol/tasks/PIG-001/theory-brief.md (831 lines; byte-identical to repo-root THEORY_001)
summary: THEORY_001's content is substantially sound and accurate where checkable, but it arrived outside the Kernel with an invalid envelope, cites three source reports that do not exist, and sets 35 acceptance criteria of which about ten cannot be objectively passed as written. Workstreams H and I are materially larger than the claimed 'bounded stabilization'; I is a new provenance capability, not an evidence pass. Recommend a scoped challenge from four specialists, not a specification.
intended_user_outcome: THEORY_001 §1 states it explicitly: 'Let a first-time visitor discover art, admire works, form and recover a local Taste Passport, understand how their actions affect it, and recognize Pigment as an atlas of relationships in art.' Assessment: the first four clauses are coherent and testable — discovery, Admire, Passport formation and recovery, and comprehension of state consequence are all observable in a browser against deterministic localStorage state, and they align with PIGMENT.md §3 (Discover→Admire→Map→Become) and §8. The fifth clause, 'recognize Pigment as an atlas of relationships in art', is coherent but NOT testable by this team: recognition is a claim about what forms in a stranger's mind, and Pigment has no approved instrumentation (PIGMENT.md §15.7; THEORY_001 §11 lists instrumentation approval as an unmade owner decision) and no authorized user research. The outcome should be split: clauses 1–4 become the acceptance-bearing objective for PIG-001; clause 5 becomes a design intent judged by a named internal adjudicator, or is deferred to the post-release Experiment 1 that THEORY_001 §13 already defines for it.
essential_principles: Atlas-first architecture is the durable core; Taste is a personal continuity layer that must never gate access to knowledge (THEORY_001 §2.1; PIGMENT.md §11).
Admire is the only positive v1 artwork taste signal; 'Seen in person' and 'Saved for later' are independent states that do not feed the taste model (PIGMENT.md §4; THEORY_001 assumptions).
The Taste Passport is accountless, local, portable, exportable and resettable, and its persistence scope must be stated literally to the user (PIGMENT.md §8; THEORY_001 §9.2).
Personas are provisional interpretation, never diagnosis, and an adopted Persona must never silently switch (PIGMENT.md §9).
Stable IDs, slugs, routes and localStorage field names are product infrastructure and may not be renamed without an alias or migration (PIGMENT.md §6; CLAUDE.md §6; THEORY_001 §4.4).
Public-domain-only imagery is a legal constraint, not a stylistic preference, and generative covers may never be presented as real artworks (PIGMENT.md §14).

Release language must be proportional to the evidence actually produced; fact, editorial interpretation, legend, disputed claim and unknown must remain distinguishable (PIGMENT.md §5; THEORY_001 §9.2, §10).
Every page must offer a meaningful onward step, and a displayed relationship must carry consequence rather than decoration (PIGMENT.md §18; THEORY_001 §5.6).
Zero-dependency static architecture: no frameworks, bundlers, npm dependencies or backend without escalation (CLAUDE.md §6; PIGMENT.md §13).
Accessibility evidence must be observed, not asserted: screenshots alone are insufficient (THEORY_001 Workstream H; CLAUDE.md Gate 2).
optional_forms: 'Start with an artist' as the visually recommended first route — THEORY_001 §2.2 explicitly declines to freeze it and §11 routes it to the product owner. Suggested form, not a requirement.

The §4.3 conceptual navigation tree (Discover / Browse the atlas / Explore relationships / Taste / Search) — THEORY_001 itself says 'This is a conceptual model, not an instruction to create routes or menus.'
Promoting Taste to persistent global navigation in this release — a live disputed point, deferred by §4.3, not a constraint.
Specific copy proposals such as replacing the search label with 'Search the atlas' (§4.2) — the principle (the label must not understate retrieval scope) is essential; this wording is a form.

The thirteen-row evidence-packet table in §8 — the principle (reproducible evidence traceable to each criterion) is essential; this layout is a form and duplicates protocol/templates/build-evidence-report.md.

The seven-width viewport list in Workstream F (320/360/390/400/768/1280/1440) — the principle (narrow, intermediate, wide, plus 200% text) is essential; 360 and 400 add little discriminating power over 320 and 390 and can be dropped with rationale.
The ten-profile sample size in Workstream I — the principle (a stratified sample with stated coverage and stated limits) is essential; the integer is a form.

The §12 ownership table's assignment of specific Claude roles, and the §12 'recommended sequence'. Per CLAUDE.md §1 the theory pole proposes and Claude owns implementation method and team composition; a theory artifact does not staff the Claude pole.
The §4.3 Atlas Coherence Pass contents — a backlog proposal, correctly deferred, and not part of this objective.
accepted_counterpole_points: The §3.1 validator snapshot is accurate. All fourteen counts and both deck warnings reproduce verbatim against today's build (intake-baseline.md §3). The theory team's data snapshot is confirmed, not stale — this materially raises the credibility of the rest of the document.

The Explore promise mismatch is confirmed in code: js/app.js:1463 promises four instruments ('Eight centuries on one timeline, an influence constellation, family trees of movements, and a world map of painters'), while js/app.js:1336 says 'Two instruments' and js/app.js:1341/1347 expose only the timeline and the influence constellation. Criterion 26 is well-founded and cheap to satisfy.

Taste is absent from persistent header navigation and present only in the footer: index.html:37-44 lists eight header destinations with no Taste; index.html:62 exposes Taste in the footer but not Explore. IVO_001.md §4.1 observed this correctly.
Stale documentation counts are confirmed (intake-baseline.md §5, C4): PIGMENT.md says 235 artists and README.md says 231 against a validator count of 246.
The malformed label in §4.2 is real: js/app.js:1795 renders 'More by ${a.name.split(" ")[0]}', which yields 'More by El' for El Greco and will misfire on every multi-token given name.
Generic platform attribution genuinely does not establish item-level rights. This is sound legal reasoning and must not be argued away for convenience.

The document's own epistemic guardrails are correct and should be preserved: 'A clean sampled console is not evidence of complete runtime stability. Absence of source proof is not proof of historical falsity or copyright infringement. Accessibility risks that were not measured remain hypotheses until tested.' (§3.3).
§0 asks Claude for analysis and classification rather than production edits, and disclaims build authorization. That is consistent with CLAUDE.md Gate 1 and is accepted.

Keeping accounts, social features, new taste mathematics, forced onboarding gates and full visual redesign out of scope (§4.4) is consistent with PIGMENT.md §2 and §11 and is accepted without dispute.
constraints_or_principles_to_defend: GENUINE — governance/architectural: no production edits before a frozen specification with workflow_state approved_for_build (CLAUDE.md Gate 1; PROTOCOL.md §6). Gate 1 is in force for PIG-001; no specification.md exists.

GENUINE — architectural constraint of the existing project: shipped IDs, slugs, routes and localStorage keys (pigment.taste.v1) are load-bearing for existing users' stored Passports and for indexed URLs. Adding fields is permitted; renaming is not.
GENUINE — safety/legal: public-domain-only imagery and honest item-level rights characterization (PIGMENT.md §14). Cannot be downgraded by implementation convenience.

GENUINE — accessibility requirement: 'A screenshot-only pass is insufficient' (Workstream H) matches CLAUDE.md Gate 2, which requires independent Quality Reviewer certification, not implementer self-certification.
GENUINE — safety/privacy: no new analytics providers or privacy-policy changes without owner approval (§4.4; PIGMENT.md §15.7).

GENUINE — architectural constraint with substantial cost consequence, NOT impossibility: this Mac has no Node.js (CLAUDE.md §6; PIGMENT.md §13), so axe-core and similar tooling are unavailable. WCAG contrast verification remains achievable via python3 computation over CSS tokens plus browser spot checks. Claude may not classify Workstream H as infeasible on this basis, and I will treat any such claim in the outgoing round as convenience presented as necessity.

REVERSIBLE IMPLEMENTATION PREFERENCE (soundly reasoned, held at the theory pole): 'preserve the current multi-door homepage as the rollback baseline' (§2.2, §10). Legitimate because no challenger has evidence, but it is a preference, not a technical constraint, and should not be described as one. Claude must not silently override it either.

REVERSIBLE IMPLEMENTATION PREFERENCE / SCOPE DECISION, not a constraint: deferring Taste from global navigation (§4.3). Note the internal tension — §3.3 names Taste's absence from persistent navigation as an observed gap while §4.3 forbids fixing it this round. Scope deferral is legitimate; it must be recorded as an accepted known gap, not reported as resolved.

SUBSTANTIAL COST/SCHEDULE CONSEQUENCE requiring repair before it is enforced: the §7 Release rule ('do not advance to human_review_ready while any applicable P0 criterion is unsupported') is sound in spirit and matches Gate 2, but combined with criteria 10, 11 and 12 it is currently unsatisfiable and therefore functions as a permanent block. See critical_issues.

WATCH LIST for the outgoing round — claims Claude might make that would be convenience disguised as necessity: (a) 'the canvas timeline and influence constellation cannot have readable alternatives' — false; a DOM/table equivalent is straightforward and is a bounded build; (b) 'no Node means contrast cannot be verified' — false, see above; (c) 'adding provenance fields would violate the stable-ID rule' — false; adding fields is not renaming IDs.

adaptations.change: The incoming task identifier THEORY-001 is carried as PIG-001 for all repository and Kernel purposes.
adaptations.reason: protocol/message-schema.json constrains task_id to ^PIG-[0-9]{3,}$, and PROTOCOL.md §7 mandates protocol/tasks/PIG-NNN/ directories. THEORY-001 cannot be represented in the system.
adaptations.constraint: Frozen schema contract (protocol/message-schema.json) and task artifact layout (PROTOCOL.md §7).
adaptations.user_consequence: None. Internal identifier only; no user-visible surface is affected.

adaptations.change: The requested workflow_state and next_state 'claude_analysis' are normalized to the legal state 'challenge'.
adaptations.reason: 'claude_analysis' does not exist in the canonical state set. The work THEORY_001 requests in §0 — feasibility classification, contradiction register, smallest reversible plan — is precisely what the 'challenge' state contains under PROTOCOL.md §1 step 2.
adaptations.constraint: PROTOCOL.md §2 state enumeration and protocol/message-schema.json workflow_state enum.
adaptations.user_consequence: None directly; it keeps the workflow auditable and keeps Gate 1 unambiguously in force, which protects the user from unauthorized production edits.

resolved_issues: C4 (stale documentation counts) is confirmed and is the cheapest item in the entire brief. Note for the Kernel: PIGMENT.md and README.md are NOT among the production paths Gate 1 enumerates (js/, css/, index.html, p/, tools/, sitemap.xml, robots.txt). Whether documentation reconciliation may therefore proceed before a frozen specification is a question for the Coordinator; I do not authorize it and do not assume it.

The THEORY_001 / THEORY_001.md duplication at repo root is a duplicate, not a conflict — both are SHA1 d3a37c422a3cff7d384667cdfc645b6c7c946663 (intake-baseline.md §2). No action needed beyond housekeeping.
§3.1's data snapshot is verified accurate and needs no re-derivation by any specialist.
Criterion 1 (validator reports no errors, all references valid) already passes today: 'ALL REFERENCES VALID' (intake-baseline.md §3).
critical_issues: CRITICAL 1 — The acceptance set combined with the Release rule defines a gate that cannot be passed. §7's Release rule blocks human_review_ready while 'any applicable P0 criterion is unsupported'. P0 includes criteria 10 and 11, which cannot be satisfied against the current data model, and criterion 12, which is a negative existential. Unless the criteria are repaired, PIG-001 can never reach human_review_ready no matter how good the build is. This must be resolved with the theory pole before any build is authorized.

CRITICAL 2 — Criteria 10 and 11 are structurally unsatisfiable, not merely expensive. Criterion 10 requires sampled influence edges to carry 'source, type, and confidence/dispute information'. js/influences.js stores three-tuples [from, to, type] (js/influences.js lines 2-9 define the format; 225 edges, 244 lines) — only 'type' exists; there is no source field and no confidence field. The file header asserts 'Every relationship is grounded in the artist bios elsewhere in the atlas', but the bios themselves carry no citations (js/artists-1.js record shape: id, name, years, born, died, nation, eras, movements, techniques, style, palette, tagline, works, life — no source field), so the provenance claim is circular. Criterion 11 requires item-level provenance and rights/license status for sampled images. js/catalog-1.js stores image:{src, page, status} where 'page' points at an English Wikipedia article rather than the Commons file page, and status:'pd' is an unsourced assertion; there is no license, author, or rights field anywhere in the catalog. Satisfying 10 and 11 requires either extending the schema across js/influences.js and js/catalog-*.js — production data edits that Gate 1 currently forbids and that §4.4's anti-drift rule discourages — or agreeing that the evidence may live in an out-of-band register. That agreement does not yet exist and is the single most important thing to settle before work starts.

CRITICAL 3 — Workstream I is a new product capability presented as an evidence gate; the 'bounded stabilization' framing does not hold for it. Three specific reasons: (a) item 5 demands 'aggregate coverage counts for sourced, uncertain, generic-attribution-only, and unresolved records' — aggregate counts are not a sample; producing them means classifying the whole corpus, roughly 246 artists plus 315 artworks plus 225 influence edges, against fields that do not exist; (b) item 6 demands 'visible differentiation between fact, editorial interpretation, legend, disputed claim, and unknown' — 'visible' means new UI affordances plus new data fields, which is a feature, not a fix; (c) the enabling schema does not exist, so this is design, migration, audit and UI work in sequence. This is a second objective wearing the clothes of an evidence gate. Recommend it be split into its own task.

CRITICAL 4 — Workstream H is half verification and half net-new build, and the halves must be separated before anyone estimates it. Genuine verification (in scope for stabilization): contrast measurement in both themes, keyboard transcripts, focus visibility and order, reduced-motion parity, route-change announcement. Net-new build (not stabilization): 'readable alternatives for essential timeline and relationship information' and 'no meaning dependent solely on ... canvas rendering'. The timeline and influence constellation are canvas-rendered generative instruments (PIGMENT.md §13), so an accessible alternative is a new parallel rendering path, not a correction. Also note there is no automated a11y tooling available (no Node.js), so every contrast and keyboard result is hand-produced.

CRITICAL 5 — The P2 family (criteria 28-35) cannot be objectively passed or failed as written and should be removed from the acceptance set. #28 ('a first-time visitor can describe Pigment as...') requires user research nobody has authorized and that THEORY_001 §13 Experiment 1 explicitly defers to post-release; it also asks the team to infer exactly what §10's 'behavioral overclaim' guardrail forbids inferring. #33 ('remains recognizably Pigment without depending only on the wordmark, dark background, or display typeface') requires a blinded-stimulus recognition study; there is no possible objective pass in this project. #30 ('at least one meaningful relationship is demonstrated rather than merely asserted') has no operational definition of 'meaningful' or of 'demonstrated versus asserted' and no named adjudicator. #34 ('no decorative relationship system is more prominent than the information it communicates') compares the prominence of a system to the prominence of information — an unquantified category comparison. #31 ('global access and guided first-time entry are distinguishable') is distinguishable by whom, against what rubric. #29 is conditional on a first action that §2.2 and §11 have explicitly not frozen, so it is currently vacuous. Mitigating fact, stated for accuracy: the §7 Release rule does not make P2 blocking. But these are published as acceptance criteria and will be reported against, so they must be either demoted to design-review heuristics with a named adjudicator (Matisse for #33/#34, Mondrian for #31/#32) or rewritten. Two are rescuable: #32 becomes testable if the five §5.1-5.5 journeys are frozen and anchor/relationship/consequence/onward-path each get an observable definition; #35 becomes testable as a regression check — every route in a frozen route inventory remains reachable within N steps from home, measured against the pre-change baseline.

CRITICAL 6 — The envelope defect must be dispositioned, not waived and not used to discard the content. THEORY_001 arrived by direct commit (3c2e9fa) with an envelope failing the frozen schema on eight counts (intake-baseline.md §5, C1). Two failures matter beyond bookkeeping. First, PROTOCOL.md §3 states a non-conforming message 'is returned by the Coordinator without processing' — if a direct commit can bypass that, the Kernel's only enforcement mechanism is precedent-broken, and the next bypass will be easier. Second, the declared message_type 'final_synthesis_and_execution_handoff' is not merely illegal, it mislabels the artifact: under PROTOCOL.md §1, final_synthesis is a CLAUDE artifact produced at step 4 after Claude's challenge, and a round-1 theory-pole message is a theory_brief. The name asserts a convergence the protocol has not reached, and 'execution handoff' implies build authorization that the document's own §0 disclaims. Content soundness and envelope validity are separate questions and both answers here are clear: the content is sound, the envelope is invalid.
noncritical_issues: C1 disposition (repairable, and Claude must not perform the repair). All eight defects are mechanical: task_id THEORY-001 → PIG-001; sender pigment-theory-team → chatgpt-theory; recipient claude-team → claude-synthesis-lead; message_type → theory_brief; workflow_state and next_state claude_analysis → challenge; source_reports moved into evidence (the schema sets additionalProperties:false); and the five absent required fields populated from the body — rationale from §14, evidence from §3.1 plus the source reports, requested_actions from §0 and §6, acceptance_criteria from §7, risks from §10. Recommended disposition: the Kernel records the out-of-band arrival as a process exception in the task record; requests a conforming re-envelope from the theory pole; or, if the theory pole cannot re-send, constructs one itself and labels it 'reconstructed by Coordinator, not authored by sender'. Claude must NOT rewrite the counterparty's envelope — that would erase the process fact and put Claude's words in the other pole's mouth. PIG-001 proceeds on the content in parallel, because the content is verified sound.

C2 disposition: adopt 'challenge' as the working state. Note that §12's recommended sequence begins 'claude_analysis → challenge', treating analysis as a distinct pre-challenge state. Under PROTOCOL.md §2 no such state exists; the analysis happens inside 'challenge'. Only the Kernel sets state; I recommend, I do not set.

C3 disposition: request NIKO_001.md, ELARA_001.md and ARGUS_001.md from the Coordinator, and treat their absence as an evidence gap, not as an accusation. Three consequences follow. (a) §14's 'The six reports converge on the following' cannot be checked for half its basis. (b) §7 criteria 9-13 rest on ARGUS-style evidence reasoning that cannot be inspected. (c) The gap is wider than three documents: IVO_001.md's own evidence list cites 'ELARA terminology review', 'VERA failure-path review' and 'ARGUS evidence audit', and THEORY_001 §12 assigns live responsibilities to VERA — a seventh contributor who is not among the six source_reports and whose review does not exist in the repo either. So IVO's evidence chain has three unavailable links of its own.

task_id alias disposition: accept and record. THEORY-001 → PIG-001 was recorded by the Synthesis Lead, not applied silently (intake-baseline.md §1), which satisfies Gate 3. Ask the Kernel to carry the alias in the task record so future theory-pole references resolve, and ask the theory pole to use PIG-001 from round 2 onward.

Criterion 2 is procedurally self-satisfying: 'resolved OR intentionally accepted with a dated rationale' can always be met by writing a rationale, so it tests Claude's paperwork rather than the product. Note that docs/ADMIRE_SPEC.md:99 gives the real rule ('for each of the 5 axes, at least 2 works ≥ +40 and 2 works ≤ −40'), which makes the resolved branch objectively testable — prefer that as the criterion.

Workstream B.3 will surface a conflict THEORY_001 does not anticipate: it asks Claude to analyze the deck warnings 'against the accepted onboarding specification', but docs/ADMIRE_SPEC.md:100 states deck delivery is adaptive while PIGMENT.md §15.2 records that the implementation chooses the full deck upfront. The specification is ahead of the implementation. Per CLAUDE.md §6 this mismatch must be surfaced, not quietly resolved in either direction.

Criterion 4 assumes a Passport 'merge' behavior distinct from import. Four occurrences of 'merge' exist in js/app.js; whether a genuine merge path exists is unconfirmed. If it does not, criterion 4 is testing an absent feature and must be reworded.

Criteria 15, 16, 20, 22 and 27 use unbounded quantifiers — 'every active control state', 'all primary actions', 'required text and controls', 'essential visualization content', 'limit and failure states'. Each is testable only once the corresponding inventory is enumerated and frozen. Without those inventories, completion is undecidable and the reviewer cannot certify Gate 2. Criterion 27 additionally names 'limit' and 'failure' states that may not exist in a static app with no backend.

Criterion 24 ('exact and meaningful matches outrank incidental substrings') is testable only against a frozen query→expected-result fixture. 'Meaningful' has no definition. Same for criterion 25. Recommend Mondrian freeze roughly twenty queries with expected top results as part of the challenge.

Criteria that are already well-formed and should be preserved verbatim: 1, 4, 5, 7, 8, 14, 17 (nested interactive elements are programmatically detectable), 19 (scrollWidth versus clientWidth is objective), 21, 23, 26. Roughly a third of the set is in good shape; the problem is concentrated in P0-evidence and P2.

§12 staffs the Claude pole from the theory pole. Per CLAUDE.md §1, Claude owns implementation method and team composition. Treat as a suggestion; note specifically that assigning Van Eyck and Vermeer work in an analysis round would compromise the independence Gate 2 requires of them.
unsupported_claims: U1 — §3.3 'the mobile primary navigation conceals most destinations behind an unannounced horizontal gesture.' No traceable source: 'swipe' appears zero times across IVO_001.md, MIRA_001.md and SOREN_001.md, and 'mobile' appears in them only as a testing request, never as an observation. The mechanism is real — css/styles.css:837 sets .main-nav{order:3;width:100%;overflow-x:auto} at a narrow breakpoint — but 'conceals most destinations' and 'unannounced' are behavioral claims requiring browser measurement. Verify: Vermeer (measurement), Mondrian (affordance judgment). Currently a hypothesis.

U2 — §3.3 'root-level horizontal overflow was directly measured on sampled desktop and mobile routes.' This asserts a measurement, and no measurement appears in any available report. It is also in tension with css/styles.css:73, which sets overflow-x:hidden at the root. Either the measurement was of a descendant element, or it does not reproduce. This claim currently drives criterion 19 and part of the blocker classification, so it must be re-measured before anyone treats it as a defect. Verify: Vermeer.

U3 — §3.3 'historical and influence claims on a sampled artist profile lacked traceable sources.' The sample is unnamed and the source report (ARGUS) is missing. The specific observation is unverifiable. The structural version is confirmed and stronger: no artist, artwork or influence record in the repository carries any citation field. Verify: Seurat.

U4 — §3.3 'generic Wikimedia Commons attribution does not establish item-level image provenance, license, or rights status.' The legal reasoning is sound and I confirmed the data shape supports the concern (js/catalog-1.js: image:{src, page, status}, where page is an English Wikipedia article rather than a Commons file page). But no report in the repository contains the audit this sentence summarizes. Verify: Seurat.

U5 — §3.3 'search ... may rank incidental substrings too highly.' Hedged, unsourced, and no query is cited. Not currently actionable. Verify: Mondrian defines a query fixture; testing follows in the build round.

U6 — §3.3 'route changes do not consistently establish concise focus and current-location orientation.' No source report. Structurally supported: aria-current occurs zero times in index.html and js/app.js, and no skip-link pattern was found in index.html, css/ or js/app.js. The full picture still needs browser observation. Verify: Mondrian, then Van Eyck at review time.
U7 — §3.3 'selected filters, modes, palette tones, and saved actions can communicate state only visually.' No source report; partially checkable by static inspection of CSS and JS. Verify: Mondrian.

U8 — §3.3 'the Start with an artist surface contains competing nested or adjacent actions.' No source report. Programmatically checkable (nested interactive elements such as anchor-inside-anchor or button-inside-anchor). Verify: Mondrian.

U9 — §14 'The six reports converge on the following.' Three of six sources are unavailable, so the convergence claim cannot be checked for half its basis. Not an accusation; a limit on how much weight §14 can carry.

U10 — §1 'Pigment has crossed the threshold from a painter reference project into a credible taste atlas.' Evaluative framing with no stated evidence standard. Harmless as prose; must not be quoted as a finding or as support for release language.

NOT unsupported, recorded for balance — §3.1's snapshot is fully verified (intake-baseline.md §3), and the Explore mismatch, the header/footer Taste asymmetry, the stale counts, and the 'More by El' defect are all confirmed in code. The document's error rate on checkable claims is low; the problem is concentrated in claims whose sources are missing.
evidence_gaps: NIKO_001.md, ELARA_001.md and ARGUS_001.md do not exist in the repository (intake-baseline.md §5, C3). ELARA and ARGUS are the presumed sources for most of the accessibility, state-communication and provenance claims in §3.3, which is exactly the set I could not trace.
VERA's failure-path review is cited as evidence by IVO_001.md and is assigned live responsibility in §12, but VERA is not listed among the six source_reports and no such document exists.

No browser observation of the current build exists anywhere in this task. Every claim about focus behavior, root overflow, mobile discoverability, contrast, reduced motion, keyboard order and 200%-text behavior is currently untested.
No provenance or rights audit exists, and the data model has no field that could hold one.
Whether a distinct Passport merge path exists (criterion 4) is unconfirmed.

Effort estimates in this packet for Workstreams H and I are reasoned from verified structural facts (corpus sizes, absent schema fields, canvas-rendered instruments) but are not measured. They are estimates and should be labelled as such downstream.

I did not verify §3.3's claims myself beyond what static inspection settles, by design — that is specialist work. Everything I inspected is cited by file and line; everything else is listed above as needing verification and by whom.
specialists_recommended: Caravaggio (Product Challenger) — PRIMARY, materially needed. This round is precisely opposition to unproven claims and scope inflation: challenge the blocker status of U1-U8, challenge the 'bounded stabilization' framing of Workstreams H and I, and challenge the P2 criteria family.

Mondrian (UX Architect) — materially needed, scoped. Produce the enumerations that turn unbounded criteria into testable ones: the control inventory for criteria 15/16, the route inventory for 14 and 35, the frozen search-query fixture for 24/25, the definition of 'essential' visualization content for 22, and observable definitions for 32. Do not design solutions this round.

Seurat (Data Steward) — materially needed, scoped narrowly to two questions only: what a provenance and rights register would have to contain given that no source, license, or confidence field exists anywhere; and what the aggregate coverage baseline in Workstream I.5 would actually cost across 246 artists, 315 artworks and 225 edges. Explicitly NOT to begin the audit — starting it before the container question is settled risks days of work in the wrong format.

Dürer (Implementation Lead) — needed in an advisory, read-only capacity only. Gate 1 forbids edits and no specification exists. Wanted for feasibility opinions on F3 (canvas alternatives), F4 (contrast verification without Node) and F5 (does merge exist). Must make no production edit.
Rubens (Synthesis Lead) — already active; consolidates the above into the challenge artifact and owns the conforming envelope.

Vermeer (Browser Evidence Reviewer) — CONDITIONAL, and the tradeoff should be decided by the Kernel rather than by me. Argument for: U1 and U2 are claims about the CURRENTLY DEPLOYED build, not about Claude's work, so measuring them does not compromise independence the way reviewing one's own pole's output would; and §0 demands a blocker/backlog classification that cannot be made honestly while its two most safety-relevant inputs are unmeasured. Argument against: it spends a build-round specialist early. If invoked, scope to exactly two measurements — document scrollWidth versus clientWidth at 320, 390 and 1280, and mobile navigation reachability at 320 and 390 — and nothing else. If not invoked, U1 and U2 stay explicitly provisional and no criterion may be classified as a blocker on their basis.

Van Eyck (Quality and Accessibility Reviewer) — NOT this round. He is the Gate-2 certifier and must be independent of the work he certifies (CLAUDE.md §2, PROTOCOL.md §6). Helping to shape the acceptance criteria he will later certify against would compromise that independence at the exact point where it matters most. Engage at internal_review.

Matisse (Visual Design Director) — NOT this round. The P2 criteria are the ones I recommend demoting out of the acceptance set, and the visual work sits in the deferred Atlas Coherence Pass. If P2 survives as design-review gates rather than tests, Matisse becomes the named adjudicator for #33 and #34 — but that is a later round.
Van Gogh (Content Editor) — NOT this round. Copy corrections follow a frozen scope; §9.1's frozen terms and ELARA's ownership of sentence-level voice make premature copy work wasteful.

human_escalation.reason_category: not_yet_ripe — material identity/purpose and scope decision expected in round 2
human_escalation.decision_required: None this round. The decision that will require the owner is release positioning: whether Pigment's next release is presented as an editorial discovery and taste product, or as a historical reference. THEORY_001 raises it as disputed point 5 and Workstream A items 4-5, and it governs how much of Workstream I is actually required — a discovery/taste positioning needs honest uncertainty language and image-rights hygiene; a historical-reference positioning needs claim-level sourcing across the corpus. The choice therefore decides whether PIG-001 is a bounded stabilization pass or two objectives.
human_escalation.why_the_teams_cannot_resolve_it: It is not yet unresolvable by the teams, which is why I am not escalating. This is round 1, analysis only; no production file has been edited and nothing irreversible has happened. The designed mechanism — Claude's challenge to the theory pole — has not been used yet, and §11 already routes this class of decision to MIRA as product-direction owner before it reaches Arda. The question also gets materially sharper once Seurat reports what the provenance baseline actually costs. Escalating now would spend the owner's attention on a question that evidence due next round will substantially narrow. Escalation becomes appropriate, and I will recommend it then, if either MIRA declines to decide, or Workstream I is about to begin without the positioning settled, or new evidence indicates active rights exposure in already-published material rather than the latent, pre-existing exposure that the current deployment already carries.

confidence.basis: High confidence in the envelope, criteria-testability and schema findings: those rest on the frozen schema text and on file-and-line inspection of js/influences.js, js/catalog-1.js, js/artists-1.js, js/app.js, index.html and css/styles.css, all cited. High confidence that §3.1 is accurate, per the Synthesis Lead's unedited validator run. Lower confidence in the scope-realism magnitude: the direction is well supported by verified structural facts, but the effort figures are reasoned estimates, not measurements. No confidence claimed for any behavioral, accessibility or provenance assertion — none of it has been observed, which is why it is routed rather than concluded.

rationale:
THEORY_001 is a better document than its arrival suggests, and the two facts should not be confused. Where it can be checked it is accurate: the validator snapshot reproduces exactly, the Explore promise mismatch is in js/app.js at lines 1463 and 1336, the header/footer Taste asymmetry is in index.html at lines 37-44 and 62, the 'More by El' defect is at js/app.js:1795, and the stale counts are confirmed. Its epistemic guardrails — that a clean console is not stability, that absent proof is not proof of infringement, that unmeasured accessibility risk is a hypothesis — are exactly right and better than most artifacts of this kind. So the content should be worked on, not returned.

Three things nonetheless prevent this from becoming a specification. First, the acceptance set cannot be passed. Criteria 10 and 11 demand source, confidence and rights data from a data model that has no such fields — js/influences.js stores three-tuples, js/catalog-*.js stores an unsourced status flag pointing at a Wikipedia article rather than a Commons file page — and criterion 12 asks for proof that no unknown is hidden, which is not a demonstrable proposition. Since the Release rule blocks human_review_ready on any unsupported P0 criterion, the gate as written is permanently closed. That is not a drafting nit; it is the difference between a release that can ship and one that cannot.

Second, the scope framing does not survive contact with the workstreams. Eight of the ten are genuine stabilization: verification with small, reversible corrections. Workstream I is not. Aggregate coverage counts across roughly 786 records are not a sample, 'visible differentiation between fact, interpretation, legend, disputed claim and unknown' is a UI feature, and both sit on a schema that would have to be designed and migrated first. Workstream H is half genuine verification and half a new parallel rendering path for canvas-drawn instruments. Calling this a bounded stabilization pass is not dishonest — I read it as the natural result of six theory reports being consolidated by a team that could not inspect the data model — but it is wrong, and it should be corrected before anyone estimates the work rather than after.

Third, three of six source reports are missing, and they are precisely the ones that would carry the accessibility, state and provenance claims I could not trace. I checked: 'swipe', 'overflow', 'rights', 'license', 'Wikimedia' and 'substring' appear nowhere in the three available reports. That does not make those claims false — several are structurally corroborated by the repository — but it does mean eight of the twelve gaps in §3.3 are currently hypotheses, and classifying any of them as a release blocker today would be fabrication dressed as diligence.

The envelope question is separate from all of this and has a clean answer. The content is sound; the envelope is invalid and could not have passed the Kernel. Both must be said. Waiving the defect would teach that direct commits bypass the protocol, which costs the Kernel its only enforcement power. Discarding sound content over an envelope would be process fetishism. So: record the exception, request a conforming re-envelope from the theory pole, and proceed on content in parallel — with Claude not touching the counterparty's envelope, because writing another pole's message is not repair.

The smallest useful response is therefore a challenge, not a specification and not the start of an audit: a conforming envelope, the contradiction register, the four-way classification §0 asks for, the testability triage of all 35 criteria with concrete rewrites, the scope finding on H and I with three named options, and three questions back to the theory pole — send the missing reports, settle the release positioning, and agree where provenance evidence may live if not in the data files. Four specialists cover that, and the two review-time roles stay out so that the independence Gate 2 depends on is still intact when it is needed.

owner_report.title: Synthesis Liaison Report — PIG-001 incoming analysis of THEORY_001
owner_report.markdown:
# Synthesis Liaison Report

**Task:** PIG-001 (arrived as THEORY-001) · **Round 1** · Incoming analysis · Analysis only, nothing built

## Current Position

The theory team sent a large handoff document asking Claude to stabilize Pigment and prove it works, rather than redesign it. Claude has changed nothing and is not authorized to: no specification is frozen, so the no-implementation gate is in force and was respected. My job this round was to read that document carefully before Claude answers it.

The short version: it is a good document that cannot be executed as written yet.

## Feasibility

The document asks for a "bounded stabilization pass". For eight of its ten work areas that is a fair description — checking that things work, with small reversible corrections.

Two areas are not that, and this is the main finding.

The provenance and rights work asks Claude to record, for every kind of record in the atlas, where the information came from and what the image rights are, and to report totals across the whole collection. Pigment's data files have nowhere to put that information — there is no field for a source, a licence, or how confident a claim is. The influence records store only who influenced whom and how, with no source at all. So this is not a checking exercise; it is designing and filling a new layer of the data, across roughly 800 records, plus new on-screen ways of showing when something is disputed or uncertain. That is a second project, not part of a stabilization patch.

The accessibility work is half genuine checking — contrast, keyboard use, focus, reduced motion — and half new building, because the timeline and influence map are drawn on a canvas and would need a readable alternative built alongside them. The checking half belongs in this release. The building half does not.

The practical consequence is that a release described as small currently cannot finish until an unbounded piece of work finishes. Recommended fix: split it, and let the stabilization release ship on its own.

## Adaptations Made

Two, both housekeeping, both already recorded rather than done silently:

1. The incoming id THEORY-001 is carried as PIG-001, because the protocol's own format requires it.
2. The requested status "claude analysis" is not a real status in this system; the equivalent real one is "challenge".

Neither affects anything a visitor to Pigment would see.

## Product Intent

Preserved. Nothing in the document redefines what Pigment is. Its stated goal — a first-time visitor discovers art, admires works, builds and can recover a personal taste record, and understands how their actions affect it — matches the product vision and is testable, with one exception: the final clause about visitors *recognizing* Pigment as an atlas of relationships is a claim about what forms in a stranger's mind. Pigment has no approved way to measure that, and the document itself defers that experiment until after release. It should be treated as a design intention, not a pass/fail test.

## Evidence

Strongest verified support: the document's data snapshot is exactly right — all fourteen figures and both warnings reproduce against today's build. Several specific faults it names are confirmed in the code: the homepage promises four exploration tools while the Explore page offers two; "Taste" is missing from the top navigation but present in the footer; a label renders as "More by El" for El Greco because it cuts names at the first space; and the documentation undercounts the artists.

Important gaps: three of the six reports this document is built from do not exist in the repository, and they are the ones that would have carried most of its accessibility and provenance claims. I checked — the words underlying eight of its twelve stated gaps appear nowhere in the three reports we do have. Those eight are reasonable suspicions, several of them supported by the code, but none is proven, and none should be called a blocker until someone measures it.

## Remaining Problems

1. The acceptance list cannot be passed as written. Of 35 criteria, roughly ten cannot be objectively judged — several ask whether a first-time visitor can *describe* or *recognize* Pigment a certain way, which needs user research nobody has authorized, and one asks Claude to prove no unknown is hidden, which is not a provable statement. Because the release rule blocks completion on any unmet criterion in that group, the finish line as written can never be crossed. This needs fixing with the theory team before any build starts.
2. Two criteria ask for source and rights information the data files have no place to store.
3. The document arrived by direct commit rather than through the coordinator, and its header does not meet the agreed format on eight counts. The content is sound and should be worked on; the arrival still needs recording, because if bypassing the coordinator carries no consequence the rule stops meaning anything.
4. A decision is coming that only you can make — see What Happens Next.

## Synthesis Assessment

Feasibility is not yet supported, and convergence is nowhere near. That is the expected state for round 1 and not a setback. What would be a mistake is treating this document as ready to build from: it would commit Claude to an open-ended audit and to a finish line that cannot be reached.

## What Happens Next

I have recommended the coordinator send this to four Claude specialists — the challenger, the UX architect, the data steward, and the implementation lead in an advisory capacity only — to produce a challenge back to the theory team. Not a build plan, and deliberately not the start of the provenance audit, which could waste days if the format question is settled afterwards. I have kept the two review specialists out of this round on purpose, so their independence is intact when they are needed to certify the work later.

That challenge should ask the theory team for three things: the three missing reports; agreement on where provenance evidence is allowed to live; and a decision on how this release is described.

That last one is the decision likely to reach you next round: whether Pigment's next release is presented as an editorial discovery and taste product, or as a historical reference. It is not a wording choice — it decides how much sourcing and rights work is genuinely required, and therefore whether this is one release or two. I am not asking you to decide it now. The theory team's product lead is meant to weigh in first, and the question gets much sharper once we know what the provenance work actually costs.

## Attention Required

No owner attention required.
